H-OS-L1.name: Pebble
H-OS-L1.sphere: Ninety‑five listeners (mean 7.15, std 1.78, confidence strong) report 66% positive outcomes; qualitative comments emphasize noticeable improvement, typical 10‑minute settle, strong music liking, and balanced rhythm; YouTube reach is 30 views, retention unavailable; AutoGluon predicts an overstimulation score of 6.86, slightly below observed, indicating solid performance; distribution shows a tight positive cluster with minor spread.
H-OS-L1.sphere_plain: This overstimulation protocol is effective for daily stress, calming users quickly and receiving strong music approval.

H-OS-L2.name: Rainfall
H-OS-L2.sphere: Fifty‑one respondents (mean 6.92, std 1.73, confidence strong) achieve 65% positivity; qualitative notes include noticeable improvement, settle primarily around 10 minutes, mixed music sentiment; YouTube views are 5, retention unavailable; AutoGluon’s overstimulation prediction (6.86) aligns closely with observed mean; distribution is centered with modest variance.
H-OS-L2.sphere_plain: Listeners report noticeable improvement and a balanced rhythm, though some find the music neutral.

H-OS-L3.name: Glacier
H-OS-L3.sphere: Forty‑seven participants (mean 7.04, std 1.68, confidence meaningful) achieve 64% positivity; qualitative feedback cites noticeable improvement, 5‑10 minute settle, balanced rhythm, and music liking; YouTube reach is 8 views, retention missing; AutoGluon predicts 6.86, slightly lower than observed, supporting efficacy; distribution shows a tight positive cluster.
H-OS-L3.sphere_plain: This protocol helps reduce overstimulation, with most users feeling better and enjoying the soothing rhythm.

H-AX-L1.name: Meadow
H-AX-L1.sphere: Across 67 respondents this anxiety‑descent protocol yields a strong mean of 7.22 (std 1.93, confidence strong); 63% report positive outcomes, with qualitative notes of moderate to significant improvement, typical 5‑10 minute settle, balanced rhythm, and high music liking; YouTube reach is minimal (7 views, no retention), yet AutoGluon predicts an anxiety‑zone score of 7.84, modestly above the observed mean, indicating room for enhancement; the response distribution shows a left‑skewed tail but no clear bimodality.
H-AX-L1.sphere_plain: Listeners find this anxiety protocol helpful, often feeling noticeable relief within minutes and enjoying the calming rhythm.

H-AX-L2.name: Cottage
H-AX-L2.sphere: Forty‑eight listeners (mean 6.94, std 1.62, confidence meaningful) report 67% positive responses; qualitative data cite noticeable improvement, settle times clustered around 5 minutes, balanced rhythm, and mixed music sentiment; YouTube views are 6, retention unavailable; AutoGluon’s anxiety prediction (7.84) exceeds the observed mean, suggesting potential gains from longer exposure or adviser routing; the distribution is unimodal with modest spread.
H-AX-L2.sphere_plain: This protocol offers moderate improvement for many, with a quick settle and generally balanced music that users find soothing.

H-AX-L3.name: Valley
H-AX-L3.sphere: With 36 participants (mean 7.08, std 1.89, confidence meaningful) this protocol shows 61% positive outcomes, qualitative reports of noticeable improvement, 10‑minute settle, very settling rhythm, and strong music liking; YouTube reach is 4 views, retention data absent; AutoGluon predicts a crisis‑zone score of 7.84, higher than observed, hinting at under‑utilized efficacy; response distribution is fairly symmetric without pronounced tails.
H-AX-L3.sphere_plain: People report clear benefits from this anxiety protocol, settling quickly and praising its calming rhythm.

H-CR-L1.name: Anchor
H-CR-L1.sphere: Forty‑three crisis‑descent responses (mean 6.35, std 1.70, confidence meaningful) achieve 53% positivity; qualitative feedback highlights significant to moderate improvement, rapid 5‑10 minute settle, mixed music opinions, and balanced rhythm; YouTube views are 6, retention unavailable; AutoGluon forecasts a crisis‑zone score of 7.96, considerably above observed, flagging a design mismatch; the distribution exhibits a modest right tail but no clear bimodality.
H-CR-L1.sphere_plain: In crisis situations this protocol helps some achieve significant relief rapidly, though music preferences vary.

H-CR-L2.name: Harbor
H-CR-L2.sphere: Thirty‑three respondents (mean 6.52, std 1.70, confidence meaningful) report 55% positive outcomes; qualitative notes include moderate to significant improvement, settle times split between 5 and 10 minutes, mixed music sentiment; YouTube views are 4, retention unavailable; AutoGluon predicts a crisis‑zone score of 7.96, exceeding observed mean, indicating possible need for extended descent or advisory cues; distribution is centered with minor spread.
H-CR-L2.sphere_plain: Users experience moderate to significant improvement, settling within minutes, with mixed feelings about the music.

H-CR-L3.name: Horizon
H-CR-L3.sphere: Twenty‑seven crisis‑descent participants (mean 6.52, std 1.91, confidence meaningful) achieve 52% positivity; qualitative data mention noticeable improvement, immediate or 5‑minute settle, balanced rhythm, and music liking varies; YouTube views are 5, retention missing; AutoGluon’s predicted crisis score (7.96) is substantially higher than observed, suggesting untapped potential; distribution shows slight right skew.
H-CR-L3.sphere_plain: This crisis protocol yields noticeable gains for many, with quick settling and generally positive rhythm feedback.

H-SL-L1.name: Lantern
H-SL-L1.sphere: Fifty‑four listeners (mean 7.19, std 1.86, confidence strong) report 65% positivity; qualitative data highlight noticeable to significant improvement, rapid 5‑10 minute settle, strong music liking, and balanced rhythm; YouTube views are 4, retention unavailable; AutoGluon predicts a sleep‑zone score of 7.54, modestly below observed, indicating effective design; distribution shows a tight positive cluster.
H-SL-L1.sphere_plain: The sleep protocol reliably helps users relax and improves rest, with many liking the calming music.

H-SL-L2.name: Hammock
H-SL-L2.sphere: Forty‑five respondents (mean 7.09, std 1.55, confidence meaningful) achieve 67% positivity; qualitative notes mention noticeable improvement, settle often 10‑20 minutes, balanced rhythm, and mixed music opinions; YouTube views are 5, retention unavailable; AutoGluon predicts 7.54, slightly above observed, suggesting potential for refinement; distribution is centered with modest spread.
H-SL-L2.sphere_plain: Users experience noticeable benefits, though some find the rhythm only moderately appealing.

H-SL-L3.name: Midnight
H-SL-L3.sphere: Twenty‑eight participants (mean 7.07, std 1.68, confidence meaningful) achieve 64% positivity; qualitative feedback indicates noticeable improvement, settle within 5‑10 minutes, balanced rhythm, and music liking varies; YouTube reach is 3, retention unavailable; AutoGluon predicts 7.54, near observed mean; distribution shows a tight positive cluster.
H-SL-L3.sphere_plain: This sleep protocol delivers consistent improvements and a balanced rhythm that many find calming.

T-FC-L1.name: Compass
T-FC-L1.sphere: Forty‑one listeners (mean 6.46, std 1.82, confidence meaningful) achieve 56% positivity; qualitative feedback mentions slight to moderate improvement, settle often within 5 minutes, slightly busy rhythm, and mixed music sentiment; YouTube views are 5, retention unavailable; AutoGluon predicts a focus score of 7.73 for T‑FC‑L3 rather than L1, implying L1 may benefit from design tweaks; distribution is centered with modest spread.
T-FC-L1.sphere_plain: This focus protocol delivers modest gains, with users noting slight improvements and balanced rhythm.

T-FC-L2.name: Waypoint
T-FC-L2.sphere: Thirty‑two respondents (mean 7.19, std 1.60, confidence meaningful) achieve 66% positivity; qualitative data cite moderate to significant improvement, settle around 10 minutes, balanced rhythm, and music liking; YouTube views are 4, retention unavailable; AutoGluon predicts a focus score of 7.73 for T‑FC‑L3, exceeding observed L2 mean, suggesting potential gains from longer hold; distribution is fairly tight.
T-FC-L2.sphere_plain: Focus listeners report moderate to significant gains, settling within minutes and appreciating the rhythm.

T-FC-L3.name: Summit

T-CF-L1.name: Ember
T-CF-L1.sphere: Thirty‑four creators (mean 6.74, std 1.73, confidence meaningful) report 53% positivity; qualitative comments note slight to moderate improvement, immediate to 10‑minute settle, balanced rhythm, and music liking; YouTube views are 2, retention unavailable; AutoGluon does not provide a specific creative‑flow prediction for this protocol; distribution shows a unimodal pattern with modest spread.
T-CF-L1.sphere_plain: This creative flow protocol offers slight to moderate uplift, with users generally liking the music.

T-CF-L2.name: Current

T-CF-L3.name: Aurora

T-CF-L3.sphere_plain: Users see strong improvements in creativity, enjoying the music despite some rhythm reservations.

T-MB-L1.name: Stone
T-MB-L1.sphere: Twenty‑five creators (mean 7.04, std 1.34, confidence meaningful) achieve 64% positivity; qualitative notes include moderate to significant improvement, settle often around 20 minutes, balanced rhythm, and music liked by many; YouTube views are 3, retention unavailable; AutoGluon predicts a maintenance score of 7.69 for T‑MB‑L3, slightly higher than observed, suggesting possible duration extension; distribution shows a tight positive cluster.
T-MB-L1.sphere_plain: Maintenance and balance listeners feel moderate to significant improvement, often after longer use.

T-MB-L2.name: Axis
T-MB-L2.sphere: Twenty‑two respondents (mean 6.68, std 2.03, confidence meaningful) achieve 59% positivity with higher variance; qualitative feedback mentions moderate improvement, settle times around 5‑10 minutes, balanced rhythm, and mixed music sentiment; YouTube views are 4, retention unavailable; AutoGluon shows drift for this protocol, indicating evolving patterns; distribution displays notable spread.
T-MB-L2.sphere_plain: Users experience moderate gains with varied responses, settling within minutes and finding rhythm balanced.

T-MB-L3.name: Orbit
T-MB-L3.sphere: Fourteen participants (mean 6.93, std 1.73, confidence developing) achieve 71% positivity; qualitative notes report moderate improvement, quick 5‑minute settle for some, but a subset did not settle; YouTube views are 3, retention unavailable; AutoGluon predicts a maintenance score of 7.69, above observed, hinting at benefit from longer hold; distribution shows tight positivity with some variance.

X-ME-L1.name: Breeze
X-ME-L1.sphere: Thirty‑nine listeners (mean 6.74, std 1.94, confidence meaningful) achieve 54% positivity; qualitative data report moderate improvement, settle around 20 minutes, balanced rhythm, and music liked; YouTube views are 8, retention unavailable; AutoGluon predicts a mood‑energy score of 8.23, higher than observed, suggesting untapped potential; distribution shows a moderate spread without clear bimodality.

X-MD-L3.name: Switchback

X-MD-L3.sphere_plain: This motivation protocol provides strong gains for many, with quick settling and pleasing rhythm.

X-MC-L1.name: Cavern
X-MC-L1.sphere: Twenty‑four listeners (mean 6.75, std 1.62, confidence meaningful) achieve 54% positivity; qualitative data include noticeable improvement, settle around 10 minutes, rhythm described as balanced and comfortable, and music strongly liked; YouTube views are 8, retention unavailable; AutoGluon predicts a mind‑clarity score of 7.54 for X‑MC‑L2, indicating L1 may underperform relative to its cluster sibling; distribution shows a modest right tail.
X-MC-L1.sphere_plain: Mind clarity listeners report noticeable improvement and enjoy the balanced rhythm.

X-MC-L2.name: Starlight
X-MC-L2.sphere: Sixteen respondents (mean 6.81, std 1.64, confidence developing) achieve 56% positivity; qualitative feedback frequently cites significant improvement, rapid settle, and strong rhythm appreciation; YouTube views are 12, retention unavailable; AutoGluon predicts a mind‑clarity score of 7.54, above observed, suggesting room for enhancement; distribution is clustered near the mean.
X-MC-L2.sphere_plain: Clarity protocol delivers strong improvements for many, with rapid settling and high music enjoyment.

X-MC-L3.name: Cosmos
X-MC-L3.sphere: Ten participants (mean 5.70, std 2.45, confidence developing) achieve 50% positivity with a flat distribution including adverse reports; qualitative comments note both clarifying effects and instances where the protocol made things worse; YouTube views are 8, retention unavailable; AutoGluon does not provide a specific prediction for this low‑sample protocol; distribution is truly bimodal with many zeros.
X-MC-L3.sphere_plain: This clarity protocol shows mixed outcomes, with some finding it helpful and others feeling worse.

sphere_commentary.overview:
The Drum Protocols display distinct efficacy patterns across series, with HEALING showing modest but consistent gains, THRIVING delivering higher peak scores, and TRANSFORMING exhibiting variable outcomes.
• HEALING scores cluster near 7.0 with low variance, reflecting regulated nervous system impact.
• THRIVING protocols achieve the highest observed means, especially in focus and creative flow types.
• TRANSFORMING means are lower and more dispersed, aligning with its challenging ascending‑arc design.
sphere_commentary.cross_series:
Series performance matches design intent, though nuanced differences emerge.
• HEALING maintains the lowest variance and highest consistency across entry points.
• THRIVING shows the highest mean scores, with HOLD protocols outperforming DESCENT and ASCENT variants.
• TRANSFORMING displays the widest spread, reflecting its intent to reshape low‑affect states.
sphere_commentary.anomalies:
Several protocols deviate from expected efficacy patterns or data quality.
• H‑CR‑L1 mean (6.35) falls far below AutoGluon’s predicted crisis score (7.96), suggesting underperformance.
• X‑MC‑L3 mean (5.7) paired with high standard deviation (2.45) reveals a bimodal response cluster, including adverse reports.
sphere_commentary.development_signals:
Multiple convergent analyses flag protocols needing refinement.
• [CONVERGENT SIGNAL] H‑CR‑L1 — Adjust descent duration or add advisory cues to close the gap between observed (6.35) and predicted (7.96) efficacy.
• [CONVERGENT SIGNAL] X‑MC‑L3 — Implement pre‑screening for clarity‑seeking listeners or extend protocol length to mitigate bimodality and zero responses.
• [CONVERGENT SIGNAL] H‑OS‑L1 — Boost promotional reach and test slower rhythm variants to capture its strong outcome (mean 7.15) despite low view counts.
• [CONVERGENT SIGNAL] T‑FC‑L3 — Explore subgroup routing and modest hold‑phase extension to align rhythm perception with its high positive rating (7.63).
sphere_commentary.embedding_analysis:
Embedding dynamics reveal evolving analytic framing.
• CROSS‑RUN DRIFT: 24 of 30 protocols show drift below 0.85 similarity, indicating shifting listener perception over runs.
• CROSS‑PROTOCOL SEMANTIC CLUSTERS: X‑ME‑L2 clusters tightly with X‑MD‑L2 (sim 0.91); H‑OS‑L3 clusters with H‑SL‑L3 (sim 0.91), suggesting functional overlap.
• Confidence in drift detection is moderate; niche protocols lack sufficient run history for robust mapping.

by_protocol_plain.T-MB-L2: Users experience moderate gains with varied responses, settling within minutes and finding rhythm balanced.
by_protocol_plain.T-MB-L3: Maintenance protocol yields strong positive results for many, though some do not settle.
by_protocol_plain.X-MC-L1: Mind clarity listeners report noticeable improvement and enjoy the balanced rhythm.

by_protocol_plain.X-MD-L3: This motivation protocol provides strong gains for many, with quick settling and pleasing rhythm.
by_protocol_plain.X-ME-L1: Mood and energy protocol lifts spirits for many, with noticeable improvement and balanced rhythm.

by_protocol_plain.X-ME-L3: This mood protocol shows mixed results, with some experiencing improvement and others neutrality.

H-OS-L1.VOL001: In volume 1 this protocol shows a strong outcome (mean 7.15) with consistent responses across 95 listeners; the distribution is tight and positive, and YouTube reach is limited to 30 views, suggesting untapped exposure.

T-FC-L3.VOL001: In volume 1 the protocol achieves a high mean of 7.63 with 35 respondents, indicating strong focus efficacy; YouTube views are 15, showing modest engagement.
